article_meta.title: PoliticsMeet the New Proud BoysThe far-right group's views and tactics are now emulated by federal agents.By Ali BrelandIllustration by The Atlantic

reasoning: The model avoided quoting errors and mostly targeted the author’s own prose, but it used non-approved bias type labels, missed several clear instances of loaded/biased author language, and included at least one questionable omission-based critique.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged items come from the author’s prose/headline; no quoted or attributed source language was treated as author bias.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Most flagged items reflect genuine slant/spin in the author’s framing; however, the claim about selection/omission (presenting Tarrio sympathetically and offering minimal counterarguments) is debatable.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed multiple clear author-level instances such as ‘catastrophic results,’ ‘relentless intimidation,’ ‘seeped into the federal government,’ and ‘wanton actions by the state,’ among others.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Used non-listed labels (e.g., Loaded Language, False Equivalence, Framing Bias, Labeling, Guilt by Association) instead of the required set; several applied labels do not map cleanly to the allowed taxonomy.
